hypothesis: Applying class-balancing strategies during training—specifically oversampling rare reaction templates or undersampling frequent ones to achieve a more uniform template frequency distribution—will disproportionately improve top-k accuracy for rare templates (frequency < 10) without substantially degrading accuracy for common templates (frequency > 10,000), and will simultaneously improve out-of-distribution generalization on the Pistachio OOD test set for template-free models such as MEGAN and RootAligned, because the models will be forced to learn generalizable chemical features rather than memorizing dominant template patterns.
